title: myriophyllum spicatum subsp. montanum
intro: Myriophyllum spicatum subsp. montanum is a subspecies of the coontail plant, a species of aquatic plant native to North America. It has been used in herbal medicine for its purported medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of this plant are said to include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity, although more research is needed to confirm these claims. Herbal preparations of Myriophyllum spicatum subsp. montanum can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although the optimal method and dosage for therapeutic use have not been established.
